START: ^You descend into an ancient underground facility. Passages branch in every direction.
entry_hall_search: ^You search the entry hall. Dust and old records.
entry_locker: ^A locker with rope, a torch, and a map.
command_hub: ^The command hub. Seven passages radiate outward.
console_search: ^The consoles hold operational logs and system status.
archive_records: ^You find operational schedules and personnel records.
north_sector: ^The north sector. Research labs and specimen storage.
lab_alpha_search: ^Experiment logs and samples. You find a cipher fragment.
lab_beta_search: ^Research notes and equipment. Interesting but not immediately useful.
specimen_vault: ^A vault of stored specimens. Cold and clinical.
vault_search: ^Specimen cases and inventory lists. You find a key among the supplies.
north_archive: ^The north sector archive. Decades of research records.
north_archive_search: ^You find classified research summaries and a personal journal.
main_workshop: ^The main engineering workshop. Heavy tools and machinery.
workshop_search: ^You find precision tools and a set of master keys.
fabrication_room: ^A room for manufacturing components and parts.
fab_search: ^Production materials and component inventory. Nothing immediately useful.
bay_search: ^Maintenance logs and spare parts. You find an amulet among the personal effects.
south_storage_search: ^Crates and supplies. Some useful, most outdated.
east_sector: ^The east sector. Data processing and communications.
server_room: ^Rows of inactive server racks. Data cores still intact.
server_search: ^The servers hold encrypted data. You extract some operational records.
comms_centre: ^The communications centre. Radios and satellite equipment.
radio_systems: ^Radio equipment with recorded transmissions. The last ones are panicked.
data_archive: ^The main data archive. Petabytes of stored information.
data_archive_search: ^Classified files and research summaries. You find a guide's personal log.
east_ops_room: ^The east operations room. Status boards and duty rosters.
east_ops_search: ^Duty logs and incident reports. The last entries describe an emergency evacuation.
security_hub: ^The security hub. Monitors, access panels, and incident logs.
security_search: ^You find access logs and a master override card.
containment_wing: ^A wing for containment of hazardous materials. Sealed doors everywhere.
containment_search: ^Hazmat equipment and containment protocols. Nothing of immediate use.
west_archive: ^The west sector archive. Security records and containment reports.
west_archive_search: ^Incident reports and containment breach records. Alarming reading.
perimeter_control: ^Controls for the facility perimeter. Fence systems and gate controls.
fence_controls: ^The fence control panels. You find a weakness in the outer fence.
central_chamber: ^The central chamber. The heart of the facility. All sectors connect here.
central_search: ^The chamber holds artefacts and equipment from every sector.
upper_level: ^The upper level. Executive and administrative areas.
executive_suite: ^The executive suite. Offices and a private conference room.
exec_office_search: ^The executive desk holds personal files and a safe. The safe is empty.
upper_boardroom: ^A large boardroom. A presentation was being prepared when the facility was abandoned.
boardroom_search: ^Presentation materials and meeting minutes. The last agenda item was marked urgent.
upper_archive: ^The upper level archive. The most sensitive records kept here.
upper_archive_search: ^Classified executive files and the facility's founding documents.
rooftop_access: ^The rooftop. A view across the facility and beyond.
rooftop_survey: ^From up here you can see the whole facility and the surrounding terrain.
rooftop_comms: ^Communications antennae and a radio transmitter on the rooftop.
lower_level: ^The lower level. Infrastructure and deep storage.
power_plant: ^The geothermal power plant. Still generating power after all these years.
generator_examination: ^Massive generators running on geothermal energy. Humming quietly.
deep_storage_area: ^Deep storage vaults. Long-term preservation of critical materials.
vault_area_search: ^The vaults contain preserved specimens, records, and equipment.
lower_maintenance: ^Lower level maintenance areas. Pipes and systems everywhere.
lower_maint_search: ^Maintenance tools and emergency supplies. A torch still works.
underground_passage_lower: ^A passage leading beneath the facility to an external exit.
resonance_corridor: ^A corridor leading to the resonance gate. The resonance key shatters in your hand.
facility_exit: ^You emerge from the facility into the open air. It is over.
null_space: ^A chamber of absolute darkness and silence. There is no exit.
crystalline_prison: ^A chamber sealed with crystal formations. No way through.
sealed_vault_wing: ^A sealed wing of the facility vault. Pristine and untouched.
phantom_gallery: ^A gallery of unknown origin within the facility.
bio_lab: ^A biological research laboratory. Sealed cabinets and sterile surfaces.
bio_lab_search: ^Research samples and experiment logs. Complex biological data.
containment_cells: ^Containment cells for biological specimens. All empty now.
cells_search: ^The cells are empty but show signs of previous occupants.
bio_archive: ^The biological sector archive. Research publications and data.
bio_archive_search: ^Research publications and a complete record of the facility's biological programme.
decontamination_room: ^A decontamination chamber between the bio sector and the rest of the facility.
physics_lab: ^A physics laboratory. Particle detection equipment and measurement instruments.
physics_search: ^Experimental data and measurement logs. Extraordinary research.
energy_core: ^The energy core room. A reactor at the heart of the facility's power supply.
core_examination: ^The reactor core is still active. A faint hum permeates the room.
physics_archive: ^The physics sector archive. Theoretical and experimental records.
physics_archive_search: ^Groundbreaking theoretical work. The research here was decades ahead of its time.
test_chamber: ^A large test chamber for physical experiments. High ceilings and reinforced walls.
test_chamber_search: ^Test equipment and experimental apparatus. Some still functional.
materials_lab: ^A materials science laboratory. Samples and testing equipment.
materials_search: ^Material samples and testing data. Various geological specimens.
geology_room: ^A geology analysis room. Core samples and seismic data.
geology_search: ^Seismic logs and geological surveys of the surrounding area.
sample_storage: ^Storage for geological and materials samples. Neatly catalogued.
transit_hub_seven: ^A transit hub connecting all facility sectors.
welfare_block: ^The staff welfare block. Canteen, gym, and accommodation.
facility_canteen: ^A large staff canteen. Industrial kitchen and dining area.
canteen_search: ^Preserved food and kitchen equipment. Some supplies still usable.
gym_search: ^Sports equipment and lockers. Personal effects of former staff.
staff_accommodation: ^Residential quarters for long-term staff. Rows of small rooms.
accommodation_search: ^Personal effects and journals. Lives interrupted mid-routine.
medical_centre: ^The facility medical centre. Treatment rooms and a pharmacy.
medical_search: ^Medical supplies and patient records. The last records describe stress and fatigue.
logistics_centre: ^The logistics and supply centre. Receiving, storage, and dispatch.
receiving_bay_log: ^The receiving bay. Loading docks and delivery records.
dispatch_area_log: ^The dispatch area. Outgoing shipments and packaging materials.
dispatch_search: ^Packaging materials and outgoing records. The last shipment was years ago.
supply_store: ^The central supply store. General consumables and equipment.
supply_store_search: ^General supplies. Some useful items among the inventory.
it_centre: ^The IT centre. Network infrastructure and technical support.
network_room: ^Network infrastructure. Routers and switches, all inactive.
network_search: ^Network diagrams and configuration records. A complete map of the facility's systems.
it_helpdesk: ^The IT helpdesk. Support tickets and equipment logs.
helpdesk_search: ^Support tickets reveal common problems. Recurring issues with containment systems.
it_data_centre: ^A data centre for the facility's computing needs.
data_centre_search: ^Computing equipment and storage arrays. The data is still intact.
it_archive: ^The IT archive. System logs and technical documentation.
it_archive_search: ^System logs reveal the sequence of events that led to the facility's abandonment.
research_coordination: ^The research coordination office. Managing all research sectors.
coord_meeting_search: ^Meeting minutes and action items. The last meeting addressed an emergency.
coord_archive_search: ^Cross-sector project files and collaborative research records.
environmental_monitoring: ^Environmental monitoring systems for the whole facility.
air_quality_station: ^Air quality monitoring. Sensors throughout the facility feed data here.
air_data: ^Air quality readings going back decades. Normal ranges throughout.
water_monitoring: ^Water quality and supply monitoring for the facility.
water_data: ^Water quality data. The supply is still clean and functional.
seismic_station: ^Seismic monitoring for geological activity beneath the facility.
seismic_data: ^Seismic logs showing minor activity consistent with the geothermal systems.
radiation_monitoring: ^Radiation monitoring for the nuclear and energy research sectors.
radiation_data: ^Radiation levels within safe parameters throughout the facility.
emergency_ops_room: ^The emergency operations room. Incident management and response coordination.
emergency_ops_search: ^Incident reports and emergency response plans. The last incident was never resolved.
emergency_storage: ^Emergency supply storage. Food, water, and medical supplies.
emergency_supply_search: ^Emergency rations, medical kits, and survival equipment.
evacuation_routes: ^Evacuation route maps for all sectors. Every exit is marked.
emergency_communications: ^Emergency communications systems. Separate from the main comms centre.
emergency_radio: ^An emergency radio transmitter. Separate power supply — still functional.
quality_assurance: ^The quality assurance department. Oversight of all research outputs.
qa_lab: ^The QA testing laboratory. Verification and validation equipment.
qa_lab_search: ^Testing equipment and verification records. Every research output was checked here.
qa_archive: ^The QA archive. All quality control records and audit trails.
qa_archive_search: ^Audit records and non-conformance reports. Several serious issues were flagged.
standards_search: ^Calibration standards and reference materials. Precise and well-maintained.
audit_office: ^The audit office. Independent oversight of the facility's operations.
audit_search: ^Confidential audit reports. The final report was never filed.
observation_tower: ^A tall observation tower within the facility grounds.
tower_top_seven: ^The top of the observation tower. A panoramic view of the entire facility.
tower_base_seven: ^The base of the observation tower. Entry control and equipment storage.
tower_base_search: ^Entry logs and equipment manifest. The tower was used for observation and communications.
tower_middle: ^The middle level of the observation tower. Communication equipment.
tower_middle_search: ^Communication relay equipment. Still functional. You could signal with this.
tower_equipment: ^An equipment room at the base of the tower. Maintenance supplies.
tower_equip_search: ^Maintenance tools and spare parts for the tower systems.
external_area: ^The external grounds surrounding the facility buildings.
east_grounds: ^The eastern grounds. Vehicle parking and loading areas.
south_grounds: ^The southern grounds. Service road and delivery areas.
south_grounds_search: ^Service road and delivery infrastructure. A route to the exit is visible.
perimeter_fence_seven: ^The perimeter fence of the facility. Security lighting and sensors, inactive.
fence_breach: ^You find a section of fence that can be passed through.
archive_central: ^The central facility archive. Master records for all departments.
classified_search: ^The most sensitive records of the facility. The truth of what happened here.
historical_records: ^Historical records of the facility going back to its founding.
historical_search: ^Founding documents and decades of operational history.
personnel_search: ^Staff files and employment records. Many hundreds of people worked here.
archive_main_search: ^The main archive stacks. General records from all departments.
connecting_corridor_a: ^A major connecting corridor linking the eastern and western halves.
connecting_corridor_b: ^A major connecting corridor linking the northern and southern halves.
junction_room_seven: ^A junction room where four corridors meet.
secondary_hub: ^A secondary hub in the outer ring of the facility.
crossroads_seven: ^A central crossroads of the facility's main corridors.
inner_ring_corridor: ^The inner ring corridor circling the central chamber.
outer_ring_corridor: ^The outer ring corridor connecting the peripheral sectors.
service_corridor_seven: ^A service corridor running parallel to the main passages.
access_corridor_north: ^A wide access corridor on the northern side.
access_corridor_south: ^A wide access corridor on the southern side.
access_corridor_east: ^A wide access corridor on the eastern side.
access_corridor_west: ^A wide access corridor on the western side.
upper_connecting_corridor: ^The connecting corridor on the upper level.
lower_connecting_corridor: ^The connecting corridor on the lower level.
facility_hub_seven: ^A central hub node tying all sections together.
